news.headline: US announces new round of Israel-Lebanon talks in Washington next week

news.summary: 'No ceasefire can be considered meaningful while children continue to be killed' says UNICEF.

news.summary: Follow our live build-up, with full team news coverage, ahead of our text commentary stream of the Group C clash.

news.headline: The UK: No. 10 Revolving Door
news.summary: UK PM Keir Starmer faces growing pressure after Andy Burnham’s by-election win.

news.headline: Iran deputy FM says ‘ready to move forward’ in deal with US
news.summary: Deputy FM Khatibzadeh tells Al Jazeera diplomacy is way forward, but US must ensure Israel stops attacks on Lebanon.

news.headline: Norway seeks to ban trade with illegal Israeli settlements in Palestine
news.summary: Norwegian government announces consultation on new bill as it steps up policies regarding 'unlawful' Israeli activities.

news.headline: Iran war: Success or disaster? Mehdi Hasan and David Des Roches

news.summary: Washington rebukes right-wing Israeli ministers for criticism.

news.headline: One person killed, several injured as two trains collide north of London

news.headline: Backlash for UK MP who linked England World Cup result to women’s safety
news.summary: Backlash for UK MP who linked England World Cup result to women’s safety.

news.headline: More than 70 medics infected with Ebola as DRC outbreak spreads ‘fast’
news.summary: Aid cuts and poor sanitation are deepening fears that Ebola is spreading through displacement camps.

news.headline: Dramatic rescue as fire rips through school in Japan
news.summary: Eyewitness video shows dozens of schoolchildren forced onto a narrow window ledge after a fire broke out in Tokyo.

news.headline: Italy’s top diplomat nixes US trip after Meloni says Trump fabricated story
news.summary: The Italian prime minister has accused Trump of making up a story that she 'begged' him for a photo at G7 in France.

news.headline: How is China using AI in the classroom?
news.summary: How will teaching artificial intelligence help China take on a high-tech future?

news.headline: Israeli air strikes hit Lebanon minutes after new ceasefire
news.summary: Minutes after a new ceasefire had come into force between Israel and Hezbollah, explosions were seen in southern Lebanon

news.headline: Two men jailed for arson attacks on property linked to UK prime minister
news.summary: The Ukrainian-born men with alleged links to Russia targeted property associated with Keir Starmer in May 2025.

news.headline: Israel continues attacks on Lebanon despite agreeing to ceasefire
news.summary: At least 47 people killed in Israeli attacks on Lebanon since midnight as US-Iran talks stall.

news.headline: Germany and Ivory Coast aim to seal World Cup knockout place: All to know
news.summary: Team news, prediction, and lineups as Germany and Ivory Coast meet in Group E aiming to secure last-32 qualification.

news.headline: ‘Destruction is the goal’: Israel steers between the US, Iran, and Lebanon
news.summary: Netanyahu faces political backlash and US criticism as Israeli attacks on Lebanon defy Iran peace agreement.

news.headline: UN welcomes fresh Lebanon ceasefire reports as rights experts urge Iran accountability
news.summary: The United Nations has welcomed reports of a new ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah on Friday while warning that civilians on the ground are still fleeing amid ongoing insecurity.

news.headline: Gaza: Sneeze and you might get shot, warns UNICEF in alert on child killings
news.summary: After another deadly night of clashes in Lebanon, aid agencies issued a new alert for Gaza, where 265 Palestinian children have been killed since a ceasefire was announced in October 2025.

news.headline: DR Congo: Efforts ramp up as Ebola outbreak accelerates beyond borders
news.summary: The spread of Ebola is accelerating in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) amid continued armed violence, posing a grave and growing risk to the region, UN agencies warned on Friday.

news.headline: UN upholds freedom of movement for peacekeepers in Lebanon
news.summary: The United Nations has again called for freedom of movement for its peacekeepers in Lebanon who continue to closely monitor developments in the south of the country, including in the wake of the recent provisional agreement signed by the United States and Iran.

news.headline: Libya’s political process regains momentum, but window for action is narrowing, UN envoy warns
news.summary: Libya has been mired in political dysfunction since the collapse of Muammar Gaddafi’s regime in 2011, which shattered State institutions and triggered recurring struggles over legitimacy and power.&nbsp;

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Gaza in the spotlight as dire conditions continue
news.summary: The Security Council debated conditions in Gaza at the request of its 10 elected members amid concern that the territory's humanitarian crisis is being overshadowed by wider regional developments. The meeting took place under a ceasefire that has existed in name since October 2025 – but nearly

news.headline: ‘Stop this madness’: Rights chief warns of impending atrocities as militia closes in on El Obeid, Sudan
news.summary: The UN’s top human rights official has issued an urgent warning that an imminent offensive against El Obeid, the capital of Sudan’s North Kordofan state, carries the risk of serious international crimes and threatens to deepen an already catastrophic humanitarian crisis.

news.headline: US-Iran deal: technical work can begin, says atomic energy agency
news.summary: The head of the UN-affiliated atomic energy agency on Thursday welcomed the signing of an initial Iran-US memorandum aimed at ending the war, before proposing “to sit down” with both parties to assist with concrete measures such as verification of Iran’s nuclear programme, a key sticking point.

news.headline: ‘Darkest chapter’: Record child violations in 2025, with national forces leading the way
news.summary: For the first time, soldiers and Government forces were responsible for more grave violations against children in armed conflict than non-State armed groups – and 2025 set a grim new record for the total number of child victims.&nbsp;

news.headline: Worsening hunger could push millions closer to famine in 13 global hotspots
news.summary: Millions of people across some of the world’s most vulnerable regions are expected to face worsening hunger in the months ahead, as conflict, economic pressures and shrinking aid budgets exacerbate long-running crises.

news.headline: Lebanon: 12 children killed, maimed daily, despite Hezbollah-Israel truce
news.summary: More than 100 days of war reveals a shattering snapshot of life for civilians in Lebanon amid Israeli strikes and forced displacement, the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF) reported on Wednesday.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Open debate on women, peace and security
news.summary: The UN Security Council met in New York Wednesday for its annual open debate on women, peace and security, in line with a 25-year-old resolution. The Secretary-General’s most recent report on the issue shows that in 2024, women made up only seven percent of negotiators on average worldwide, and near

news.headline: Ebola treatment capacity expands in DR Congo as WHO issues new guidelines
news.summary: Treatment capacity for the rare and deadly Bundibugyo species of Ebola is expanding in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), as the World Health Organization (WHO) warns that community mistrust remains a major challenge to ending the outbreak.

news.headline: A turning point for Haiti? New security force takes fight to powerful gangs
news.summary: Passengers look out of the back of a brightly-coloured shared pick-up style taxi, known locally in Haiti as a “tap tap” as it makes its way along the Boulevard du 15 Octobre in the east of the capital, Port-au-Prince.

news.headline: UN officials call for urgent action in Yemen to push peace, reduce hunger
news.summary: Top UN officials called for bolstered efforts to stave off rising hunger and foster a path towards permanent peace in Yemen as they briefed the Security Council Tuesday morning.

news.headline: ‘Some question if Ebola is real’: How trust is central in fighting DRC outbreak
news.summary: In Ebola-stricken Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), winning the race against the disease requires earning the community’s trust first and foremost, humanitarians said on Tuesday.

news.headline: World News in Brief: Risky return home in Lebanon, displacement in Gaza, emergency funding for Somalia
news.summary: Despite the agreement announced on Sunday between the United States and Iran, displaced people in Lebanon continue to face difficulties in returning to their communities, the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA) has reported.&nbsp;

news.headline: Ukraine: Latest Russian attack kills civilians, damages cultural landmark
news.summary: Several civilians were killed and dozens more were injured in the latest wave of overnight attacks in Ukraine that targeted the capital Kyiv, the city of Kharkiv and the country’s history and cultural heritage, the United Nations said on Monday.

news.headline: While world waits for details on Iran-US accord, UN calls for Hormuz aid corridor
news.summary: As representatives of Iran and the United States reportedly prepared to sign a new peace agreement at the end of the week, the UN on Monday stressed the urgent need to open an aid corridor to transit the choked-off Strait of Hormuz and prevent a global hunger crisis.

news.headline: WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation
news.summary: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) has praised Uganda’s response to an Ebola outbreak that has spread from neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while warning that continued vigilance and cross-border cooperation will be critical to stopping transmission.

news.headline: Sudan civilians trapped by fear, disappearance and detention: rights experts
news.summary: As Sudan’s conflict enters a fourth year, civilians are increasingly trapped not only by frontline violence but by fear, disappearance and detention, according to an update by the Independent International Fact-Finding Mission for the Sudan to the 62nd Session of the UN Human Rights Council in Genev

news.headline: Guterres welcomes US-Iran peace deal as ‘critical step’ toward ending conflict
news.summary: UN Secretary General António Guterres welcomed on Sunday a new peace deal between the United States and Iran, calling it a “critical step” toward ending the conflict.

news.headline: Refugee turned peacemaker: The young woman leader transforming communities in the Central African Republic
news.summary: In the heart of the Central African Republic (CAR), a nation long marked by conflict, Nina Mireille Yankinon stands as a symbol of resilience, leadership, and hope, dedicating her life to helping communities torn apart by war.&nbsp;

news.headline: Instability, war and closed borders: How aid workers get emergency food to hungry Afghan children
news.summary: The odyssey begins in Indonesia and ends, 15,000 kilometres later, in Kabul, the capital of Afghanistan. Along the way, critical food aid destined for young Afghan students crosses nine countries, over land and sea, skirting geopolitical unrest and conflict zones.

news.headline: Security Council weighs future of UN war crimes mechanism as closure nears
news.summary: The Security Council on Friday weighed the future of the UN mechanism responsible for completing the remaining work of the international tribunals for Rwanda and the former Yugoslavia, as members debated how to preserve its legacy while bringing its mandate to an orderly close.

news.headline: Women and girls caught up in Yemen’s ‘forgotten crisis’ bear the heaviest toll as funding falls
news.summary: Yemen remains gripped by one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises, with 22 million – out of a population of 35 million – requiring assistance. Women and girls account for half of those in need, and two-thirds of them are of childbearing age, placing reproductive health at the heart of the emerge

news.headline: More strikes impact Lebanon hospitals as humanitarian situation deteriorates
news.summary: The humanitarian situation in Lebanon is being pushed further into crisis as hostilities continue between Israeli forces and Hezbollah militants – and essential services and healthcare are targeted.

news.headline: UN ‘encouraged’ by talk of possible US-Iran ceasefire deal
news.summary: The United Nations continues to monitor developments amid conflicting reports on Friday of a possible ceasefire deal between the United States and Iran, the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General said in New York.

news.headline: DR Congo: Ebola spreads as agencies brace for child victims
news.summary: The deadly Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is continuing to spread with a spike in child infections an increasingly likely scenario in the days ahead, UN agencies said on Friday.

news.headline: Ukraine civilian casualty toll in May highest in four years, UN monitors say
news.summary: More civilians were killed and injured in Ukraine in May than in any other month in the past four years, UN investigators said on Friday in their latest update.

news.headline: ‘Grave concern’ after dozens of women arrested in Afghanistan for dress violations
news.summary: Gender equality agency&nbsp;UN Women is “gravely concerned” by the arrest of at least 30 women in Herat city last weekend in Afghanistan for allegedly violating dress requirements imposed by Taliban authorities.&nbsp;&nbsp;

news.headline: Three seafarers killed in Hormuz strike as UN warns of widening fallout
news.summary: Three Indian seafarers were killed in an attack on an oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, as renewed hostilities in one of the world’s most critical shipping corridors once again heightened concern over food security, fuel prices and broken global supply chains.

news.headline: Middle East LIVE: Diplomacy in focus as escalation ‘reverberates across borders and continents’, warns UN chief
news.summary: Nearly four months after the latest Middle East crisis erupted and despite a fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran, tensions continue to reverberate across the region and beyond. The UN Security Council held a high-level debate on advancing political solutions in the Middle East on We

news.headline: ‘The day never stops’ for aid workers braving missiles and drones in Ukraine
news.summary: As Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine enters its fifth year, conditions in southern Ukraine have rapidly deteriorated. Maintaining humanitarian operations has become an increasingly complex balancing act between enabling aid delivery and managing risk.

news.headline: Fresh strikes on Tyre kill eight, as UN puts Lebanon destruction bill at $365 million, and rising
news.summary: Lebanon has suffered more than $365 million in damage to buildings across Beirut and Mount Lebanon since the latest escalation, according to a new UN-led assessment released on Tuesday, as fresh strikes in Tyre underscored the fragility of a ceasefire that has failed to stop the fighting.

news.headline: Palestinians face systematic abuse by Israeli settlers and Hamas alike: Independent investigators
news.summary: Palestinian civilians are trapped between escalating settler violence in the occupied West Bank and fear-based Hamas rule in the Gaza Strip, investigators appointed by the UN Human Rights Council said on Tuesday in a new report.

news.headline: Sudan war: Drone attacks damage key aid routes
news.summary: Escalating attacks on bridges, roads and other civilian infrastructure in Sudan are disrupting humanitarian access and putting civilians at further risk, the United Nations said on Tuesday.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Central Africa in the spotlight, including outlawed Lord's Resistance Army
news.summary: The Security Council meets this morning to discuss the situation in Central Africa, including efforts to combat the so-called Lord’s Resistance Army (LRA), one of the region’s longest-running security threats, responsible for over 100,000 deaths, the UN estimates. Ambassadors have been briefed by th

news.headline: Building trust and lab testing at the heart of DRC Ebola response: WHO
news.summary: In Ebola-stricken eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) a massive push for early testing and contact tracing is helping to&nbsp;contain the virus, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) said on Tuesday.

news.headline: ‘Rare, untreatable strain’: Ebola toll mounts in eastern DR Congo
news.summary: The top UN aid official in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is in Ituri province – the epicentre of the country's Ebola outbreak – for a three-day assessment visit, as the confirmed case count reaches 515 across three eastern provinces.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: UN officials warn humanitarian toll in Ukraine is worsening
news.summary: The Security Council met on Monday amid a sharp escalation in hostilities across Ukraine, where UN officials warned that the war has reached its deadliest point since Russia’s full-scale invasion in 2022. Briefing members, Rosemary DiCarlo, Under-Secretary-General for political affairs, said recent

news.headline: Afghanistan faces ‘lost generation of talent and potential,’ Security Council hears
news.summary: As the Security Council met on Afghanistan, senior officials and civil society representatives delivered a clear warning Monday: despite relative security under the Taliban, worsening humanitarian conditions, restrictions on women and growing economic pressures are creating a fragile and uncertain f

news.headline: Deadly quake strikes Philippines on first day of school year
news.summary: A powerful earthquake struck the southern Philippine island of Mindanao on Monday morning just as millions of children were returning to school after the summer break.

news.headline: Children are dying as US sanctions push Cuba to the brink, warns UN human rights chief
news.summary: Children are dying because doctors cannot access essential medicines, UN human rights chief&nbsp;Volker Türk&nbsp;said in a&nbsp;stark warning&nbsp;on Monday, calling for the immediate lifting of United States sanctions against the Caribbean nation that were causing “widespread harm”

news.headline: From firefights to football matches: Life on a UN peacekeeping frontline
news.summary: Night has fallen over the town of Zémio, in the east of the Central African Republic. In a few hours, the December 2025 presidential election is due to take place, but the rebels of the “Azande Ani Kpi Gbe” (AAKG) militia have launched an offensive to seize the city and derail the polls.

news.headline: ‘The ocean has no boundaries’: Beauty and life in a war zone
news.summary: When US and Israeli forces launched strikes on Iran on 28 February, triggering one of the most serious geopolitical crises in years, the Strait of Hormuz – a narrow channel just 34 kilometres wide at its narrowest point – became a global flashpoint overnight.

news.headline: Lebanon crisis: Needs soar as UN launches new funding appeal
news.summary: The UN in Lebanon appealed for an additional $331.5 million on Friday to help 1.4 million people in crisis as already massive needs continue to grow, three months since deadly violence erupted between Hezbollah fighters and Israeli forces.

news.headline: Faith, fear and trust: Inside DR Congo’s fight against Ebola
news.summary: In a village in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), health workers arrived a few days ago to help bury a person who had died from Ebola. Instead, they were threatened, told armed rebels would be called if they stayed, and forced to leave.

news.headline: Haiti: Harrowing needs must be met with long-term engagement
news.summary: Escalating gang violence across Haiti has pushed displacement to record levels, deepening an already severe humanitarian crisis and leaving nearly 1.5 million people without a stable place to live, according to new figures released Friday by the UN migration agency, IOM.

news.headline: Building trust on patrol: One police officer’s peacekeeping journey in South Sudan
news.summary: From a police station in Germany to the UN Mission in South Sudan, Stephanie Königs has been talking to UN News about her journey to becoming a peacekeeper – and the importance of trust and empathy in the field.&nbsp;

news.headline: Killed for speaking up: UN sounds alarm over attacks on environmental defenders
news.summary: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk called on Thursday for greater protection for environmental and land defenders, noting that hundreds worldwide have been killed or detained in recent years.

news.headline: Yemen: Hunger crisis deepens as funding cuts leave millions without support
news.summary: Nearly half of the population in Government-controlled areas of Yemen are facing high levels of acute food insecurity with the crisis set to deepen further if international aid cuts continue, according to the&nbsp;latest analysis by&nbsp;the leading UN-backed global food security platform.

news.headline: ‘We are catching up’ – WHO chief on DR Congo’s Ebola fight
news.summary: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) said on Wednesday that the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is showing signs of progress – but significant challenges remain in testing, surveillance, vaccine development and building community trust.&nbsp;

news.headline: Growing up with sirens: UN child rights envoy on the toll of the Ukraine-Russia war
news.summary: Children in Ukraine have been profoundly impacted by years of war, sheltering in underground schools – or forced to study online – and living with the psychological strain of constant air raid sirens that could spell death for them and their families.

news.headline: Regional health agency issues measles alert for World Cup
news.summary: As World Cup fever rises in the Americas, countries are urged to strengthen measles surveillance and vaccination amid ongoing outbreaks across the region.&nbsp;

news.headline: UN deplores another wave of Russian attacks across Ukraine
news.summary: Overnight attacks in three key cities in Ukraine have left several civilians dead, scores more injured, and homes, hospitals and shops destroyed or damaged, the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country said on Tuesday.&nbsp;

news.headline: UN urges world not to forget Rohingya refugees as aid cuts threaten vital support
news.summary: As the world approaches nine years since the mass displacement of Rohingya refugees from Myanmar into Bangladesh, the UN refugee agency (UNHCR) has appealed to the international community not to abandon the 1.2 million refugees living in the country, most of them in camps in Cox’s Bazar.

news.headline: Haiti: UN rights office welcomes new judicial units to tackle gang violence and impunity
news.summary: The UN&nbsp;human rights office, OHCHR,&nbsp;on Tuesday welcomed the opening of two specialised judicial units in Haiti, saying the move marked&nbsp;“a significant step toward tackling widespread impunity in the country.”&nbsp;

news.headline: DR Congo Ebola outbreak: Nurses discharged after full recovery
news.summary: Four nurses who fell ill with Ebola&nbsp;in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) have been discharged from hospital after recovering from the often-fatal illness that sparked an international health alert.&nbsp;

news.headline: UN stresses the need for de-escalation in Ukraine war as attacks rise
news.summary: The United Nations continues to warn against the dangerous escalation in the war in Ukraine, a senior official told the Security Council on Monday, underlining the need for restraint and dialogue.&nbsp;

news.headline: Under fire, Kharkiv is already building for a peaceful tomorrow
news.summary: Every day in Kharkiv begins with uncertainty: air raid sirens interrupt sleep; missiles strike residential neighbourhoods, industrial sites, and roads. Anxious citizens rush into metro stations during bombardments and children study underground.

news.headline: ‘You are not alone’: WHO chief vows to stand with DR Congo through Ebola outbreak
news.summary: Community trust will be decisive in bringing the rapidly evolving Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo under control, WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said on Saturday, as health teams race to contain the emergency that has spread across multiple provinces and in

news.headline: ‘A disease you get when you care for someone’: WHO on the Ebola frontline
news.summary: Two weeks into the latest deadly Ebola outbreak, the World Health Organization (WHO) estimates there are now 906 suspected cases of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), including 223 suspected deaths.

news.headline: UN warns Ukraine war risks spiralling ‘out of control’
news.summary: The United Nations on Thursday warned of a dangerous escalation in the war in Ukraine after a wave of large-scale Russian strikes and threats of further attacks, with Secretary-General António Guterres saying “the death spiral must stop.”

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Mounting attacks and Russian threats prompt emergency Ukraine meeting
news.summary: The UN&nbsp;Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Ukraine, with Secretary-General António Guterres telling ambassadors following what Kyiv describes as the most devastating attack on its capital to date, that “the time for peace is now.” In the early hours of 23-24 May, Russia laun

news.headline: Ukraine: Russian attack destroys humanitarian food aid in Dnipro
news.summary: The World Food Programme (WFP) has deplored a Russian attack on a warehouse in Dnipro, Ukraine that destroyed “a significant quantity” of food aid destined for thousands in frontline areas.

news.headline: Massive Russian strike on Kyiv: ‘I hope my friends are alive’
news.summary: The overnight Russian attack on Ukraine, which centred on the capital, Kyiv, reportedly involved some 90 missiles, including a powerful hypersonic ballistic missile and 60 drones. The senior UN official in the country called for an end to civilian harm.

news.headline: From Kharkiv to the UK’s health service: Ukrainian doctor rebuilds her life – and helps others do the same
news.summary: On 23 February 2022, in her home city of Kharkiv, Dr Inna Soldatenko finished work, collected her daughter from school, cooked dinner and prepared a lecture for her students. The next morning, she awoke to explosions.

news.headline: Ukraine: UN alarmed by reports of deadly strike on dormitory in occupied Luhansk
news.summary: The United Nations voiced alarm on Friday over reports of an overnight attack on a vocational school and dormitory in the town of Starobilsk in Ukraine’s Luhansk region which killed and injured multiple civilians, including children.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Human cost of Ukraine war, a pattern that ‘defies’ international law, says senior UN aid official
news.summary: The Security Council is meeting at the request of Russia which on Friday accused Ukraine of targeting a student dormitory overnight in the occupied Luhansk region, reportedly killing six people – including children – and injuring dozens. Kyiv has denied targeting the civilian building, saying it had

news.headline: Haitian children ‘paying the highest price’ amid surge in gang recruitment
news.summary: As gangs continue to “terrorise” communities in Haiti, children are the ones paying the highest price, the UN Special Representative for Children and Armed Conflict warned on Thursday.

news.headline: 30,000 people flee fresh violence in Haiti as hunger crisis deepens
news.summary: Around 30,000 people have had to flee their homes in the Haitian capital Port-au-Prince and surrounding areas following a new wave of violent attacks and atrocities perpetrated by armed gangs over the past ten days.

news.headline: Ukraine war ‘becoming deadlier by the day’, Security Council hears
news.summary: The war in Ukraine, now well into its fifth year, “is becoming deadlier by the day”, a senior UN official warned in a briefing to the Security Council on Tuesday.&nbsp;

news.headline: Extreme heat, floods and drought threaten lives across Latin America and Caribbean
news.summary: Record-breaking temperatures, deadly floods, worsening drought and intensifying hurricanes are placing millions of people across Latin America and the Caribbean at growing risk of hunger, displacement and water shortages,&nbsp;according to a new report from the World Meteorological Organization

news.headline: Turning silence into stories: Cambodian activist amplifies LGBTQI+ youth voices
news.summary: Cambodian activist Panha Theng, a 2025 UN Young Leader for the Sustainable Development Goals, says visibility and safe spaces remain critical for LGBTQI+ youth across Southeast Asia amid continuing stigma and discrimination.

news.headline: Afghan mothers and children face worsening hunger crisis, WFP warns
news.summary: Afghanistan’s deepening malnutrition crisis is pushing mothers and children to the brink, the UN World Food Programme has warned, as mass returns from neighbouring countries and severe funding shortfalls overwhelm already strained humanitarian operations.

news.headline: Blackouts and shortages disrupt healthcare across Cuba
news.summary: Hospitals across Cuba are suspending surgeries, struggling to keep lifesaving equipment running and facing severe medicine shortages as blackouts and fuel shortages push the country’s healthcare system deeper into crisis, senior UN officials warned on Friday.

news.headline: Russian attacks on Ukrainian civilians are a clear violation of humanitarian law, says senior UN official
news.summary: The UN Humanitarian Coordinator a.i. in Ukraine, Bernadette Castel-Hollingsworth, has reacted strongly to Russian military strikes on a civilian area of Kyiv on Thursday.&nbsp;

news.headline: UN rights chief warns abuses in DPR Korea remain a ‘human rights crisis’
news.summary: Serious human rights violations continue in the Democratic People’s Republic of Korea (DPRK) and must not be allowed to fade from international attention, UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk warned on Wednesday.

news.headline: Afghanistan crisis deepens as record returns, drought and aid cuts strain economy
news.summary: Afghanistan’s humanitarian and economic crisis is deepening despite modest economic growth, with nearly three in four people unable to meet basic needs, the UN Development Programme (UNDP) said on Wednesday.
